title: Popular Subscription Plans
description: You are given three tables: plan categories, plans, and subscriptions. Find names of plans that have more than 2 active subscriptions (active = 1), ordered alphabetically.
inputFormat:
categories(id, name)
plans(id, name, category_id, monthly_price)
subscriptions(id, plan_id, subscriber, active)
outputFormat: One plan name per line, ordered alphabetically.
constraints: Use JOIN and GROUP BY. Filter WHERE active = 1. Use HAVING COUNT(*) > 2. Include ORDER BY name. Only SELECT queries are allowed.
sampleSolution: SELECT p.name FROM plans p JOIN subscriptions s ON p.id = s.plan_id WHERE s.active = 1 GROUP BY p.id, p.name HAVING COUNT(*) > 2 ORDER BY p.name;
hint: Join plans with subscriptions, filter active ones, GROUP BY plan, HAVING COUNT(*) > 2.

testCases.setupSql: CREATE TABLE categories (id INTEGER, name TEXT); CREATE TABLE plans (id INTEGER, name TEXT, category_id INTEGER, monthly_price INTEGER); CREATE TABLE subscriptions (id INTEGER, plan_id INTEGER, subscriber TEXT, active INTEGER); INSERT INTO categories VALUES (1,'Streaming'),(2,'Cloud'); INSERT INTO plans VALUES (1,'Basic',1,9),(2,'Premium',1,19),(3,'Pro',2,29); INSERT INTO subscriptions VALUES (1,1,'user1',1),(2,1,'user2',1),(3,1,'user3',1),(4,2,'user4',1),(5,2,'user5',0),(6,3,'user6',1),(7,3,'user7',1);

CREATE TABLE categories (id INTEGER, name TEXT); CREATE TABLE plans (id INTEGER, name TEXT, category_id INTEGER, monthly_price INTEGER); CREATE TABLE subscriptions (id INTEGER, plan_id INTEGER, subscriber TEXT, active INTEGER); INSERT INTO categories VALUES (1,'Music'),(2,'Video'); INSERT INTO plans VALUES (1,'Solo',1,5),(2,'Family',1,15),(3,'Ultimate',2,25),(4,'Starter',2,8); INSERT INTO subscriptions VALUES (1,1,'a',1),(2,1,'b',1),(3,1,'c',1),(4,1,'d',1),(5,2,'e',1),(6,2,'f',1),(7,3,'g',1),(8,3,'h',0),(9,4,'i',1);
